---
title: "Client Report - Exploring Names"
subtitle: "Unit 1 Task 1"
author: "Mitzi Vite"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
    
---

```{python}
import polars as pl
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```

```{python}
# Learn morea about Code Cells: https://quarto.org/docs/reference/cells/cells-jupyter.html

# Include and execute your code here
df = pl.read_csv("https://posit.byui.edu/content/0d43d66f-d3b7-4c43-8c36-52e3ed69e7f1/names_year_csv.csv")
# df.columns
df.head()

```

## QUESTION 1

__What was the earliest year that the name ‘Felisha’ was used?__

_After sorting by year, the result shows the earliest year that the name 'Felisha' was use is 1964._

```{python}
# Q1
df.filter(pl.col('name')=='Felisha').sort('year').head(1)\
  .select(['name', 'year'])
```


## QUESTION 2

__What year had the most babies named 'David'? How many babies were named 'David' that year?__

_The year with the most babies named 'David' was 1992, with 86,389 babies named 'David'._

```{python}
# Q2
df.filter(pl.col('name')== 'David').sort('Total', descending=True).head(1)\
  .select(['name', 'year', 'Total'])
```


## QUESTION 3

__What year did your name hit its peak? How many babies were named your name in that year?__

_The year with the most babies named 'Mitzi' was 1995, with 437 babies named 'Mitzi'._

```{python}
# Q3
df.filter(pl.col('name')=='Mitzi').sort('Total', descending=True).head(1)\
  .select(['name', 'year', 'Total'])

```


## QUESTION 4

__In the most recent year, what was the most common female name in Utah?__ 

_The most common female name in Utah in 2024 was Olivia, with 215 occurrences._

```{python}
# Q4
df.filter(pl.col("year") == df["year"].max()) \
  .sort("UT", descending=True) \
  .select(["name", "year", "UT"]) \
  .head(10)

```


---
